Now I have a clear view of atom 11 (C) and all its covalent bonds. From the zoomed-in image, I can identify all the atoms directly bonded to atom 11.

Here are my findings:

## Atom 11 (C) — Neighbour Atoms

Atom 11 (C) is part of an aromatic ring and is covalently bonded to **three** neighbour atoms:

| Atomic Index | Element |
|:---:|:---:|
| 10 | C |
| 12 | C |
| 16 | C |

**Explanation:** Atom C:11 sits in an aromatic (phenyl) ring. It is bonded to:
- **C:10** — the carbon connecting toward the O:9 / C:8 side of the molecule
- **C:12** — the adjacent ring carbon (below, bonded to H:21)
- **C:16** — the adjacent ring carbon (above, bonded to H:17)

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 11, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
